text: In a previous video, I have shown that the motor branch hooks around the ligamentum arteriosum and heads up to the right side under the arch of the aorta.
It then joins the tracheoesophageal groove at a level higher than the reflection point.
Our target node was at the level of the carina or the tracheobronchial junction.
There is a good 2 inches between the carina and the reflection point of the recurrent laryngeal nerve, making it safe to biopsy the target node.
Here is an example of 4L node similar to our target node but in a different patient.
Proper reading of the CT and PET scans allows the surgeon to improvise new approaches, as in this case.
We then harvested station 10, station 7, and we are about to start harvesting stations 2 to 4.
Note is made of the superior vena cava and the phrenic nerve over it and the joining azicus arch.
Also make note of the main stem trachea with the vagal trunk over it.
The oesophagus is posterior to the main stem trachea.
The medial pleural investment is opened over the superior triangle.
This is a triangle made by the phrenic nerve, the vagus nerve and the azicus arch.
The pleural investment is open where the azicus joins the SVC and a trapdoor is opened to expose the vagus.
The borders of the fibrofatty nodal pack is then defined and the pack is dissected en masse using the bipolar device Enseal.
The vagus nerve is then slung using a sloop or a suture of your choice and retracted out of the way.
The azagus arch is similarly sloped and retracted downwards to expose the tracheobronchial junction, which is our landmark for the carina.
This should be just above the azagus arch.
A ring forceps is then dug just behind the trachea at that point and opened widely.
This will be the tunnel that will lead us to our target node.
And indeed, once the space between the trachea and oesophagus is opened, the target node is clearly seen without a shred of doubt.
The node is then teased out with the help of an endopinot and an enceal bipolar dissection.
Bleeding from the feeding vessel is not usually a problem and can be easily controlled.
The Enseal energy device should be used very close to the node to avoid injury to nerves.
At the end of harvesting, the space is checked for bleeding and hemostasis is secured before closing the ports over a single 28 French drain.
